current_wind.description: SE wind at 7.3 mph, gusts to 16.8 mph

bh.shot_strategy: For RHBH, aim slightly left of the basket and throw with a controlled hyzer using a stable to understable disc. This strategy will help keep the disc from drifting right into the roadway OB, as the 2 o'clock wind will push it that way.

fh.shot_strategy: For RHFH, throw with a slight hyzer using a stable to overstable disc. This helps counter the wind pushing from the right, ensuring the disc stays on a line towards the basket and away from the OB.

bh.shot_strategy: For LHBH, a neutral or slightly anhyzer release with an understable to neutral disc is recommended. The wind from the right will help push the disc back towards the fairway, away from the OB on the right.

fh.shot_strategy: For LHFH, focus on a clean, straight throw with a stable disc, as the wind from the right combined with the natural fade could push the disc OB to the right.

wind_info.description: Crosswind from the left pushing right

bh.shot_strategy: For RHBH, throw with a strong hyzer angle using an overstable disc to fight the crosswind from the left, keeping the disc from drifting right into the OB.

fh.shot_strategy: For RHFH, a flex line with a stable to understable disc will work well, utilizing the wind from the left to push the disc towards the right side of the fairway, away from the OB.

bh.shot_strategy: For LHBH, a significant hyzer with an overstable disc is crucial to fight the combined force of the natural fade and the wind pushing right, ensuring the disc stays left of the OB.

fh.shot_strategy: For LHFH, focus on a straight, controlled throw with a stable disc, perhaps with a slight hyzer to keep the disc from drifting too far right and potentially out of bounds.

bh.shot_strategy: For RHBH, throw with a slight anhyzer using an understable to stable disc to counter the wind pushing from the left, keeping the disc on a line towards the basket.

fh.shot_strategy: For RHFH, a straight or slightly hyzer release with a stable disc will help counteract the wind pushing from the left, keeping the disc from drifting too far right.

bh.shot_strategy: For LHBH, a straight or slight hyzer release with an understable to neutral disc will be effective, as the wind from the left will help keep the disc from fading too far right.

fh.shot_strategy: For LHFH, a slight anhyzer release with an understable disc is recommended to counter the wind pushing from the left, helping the disc track towards the basket.

bh.shot_strategy: For RHBH, throw with a stable disc on a hyzer angle to fight the strong wind pushing from the left and keep the disc from drifting too far left.

fh.shot_strategy: For RHFH, a controlled, straight release with a stable disc is best to manage the strong crosswind from the left, preventing excessive drift to the right.

bh.shot_strategy: For LHBH, a straight or slight hyzer shot with an understable to neutral disc will be effective, as the wind from the left will help bring the disc back towards the center if it fades.

fh.shot_strategy: For LHFH, a slight anhyzer with an understable disc is recommended to counter the strong wind pushing from the left, helping the disc track towards the basket.

bh.shot_strategy: For RHBH, use an understable disc and throw with a slight hyzer, ensuring you stay on the island, to take advantage of the tailwind and keep the disc from drifting left out of bounds.

fh.shot_strategy: For RHFH, throw with a stable disc on a hyzer angle, letting the tailwind assist with distance while keeping the disc from drifting too far left and out of bounds on the island.

bh.shot_strategy: For LHBH, an understable disc on a hyzer line will be effective, as the tailwind will help carry it forward, and the hyzer will counter the natural fade to the right, keeping it on the island.

fh.shot_strategy: For LHFH, a controlled anhyzer release with an understable disc is recommended to maximize distance with the tailwind and keep the disc from drifting left out of bounds on the island.
